title: refactor(gen-middleware): consolidate not-billed guards and drop dead cache-header parsing
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
Consolidate the not-billed/content-type guards in `trackResponse` and drop dead semantic-cache parsing (gen middleware):

- Add a `notBilled()` helper for the 5 repeated not-billed early-returns; factor the 3 content-type guard blocks into one `getContentTypeGuard()` — preserving the `streamRequested` gate, startsWith-vs-includes per branch, the audio STT model lookup, and a `{kind}` warn
- `extractCacheHeaders`/`CacheData` reduced to `{ cacheHit, cacheKey }`; remove the always-undefined `cacheType`/`cacheSemanticSimilarity`/`cacheSemanticThreshold` (the `x-cache-*` headers fail zod parse → undefined in every emitted event). Tinybird datasource columns left intact (separate destructive deploy)
- **Adds 2 regression tests** for the wrong-content-type non-billing paths (JSON for `generate.image`, non-SSE for streamed `generate.text`) — asserting `isBilledUsage:false` + still-200. These paths were previously untested.

Net −15 source lines; biome + tsc clean; tracking/error-observability suites 9/9 (baseline 7 → +2 new).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Spring cleaning the tracking middleware
gist.blurb: Tidied up the generation tracking middleware by consolidating billing guards and dropping some dead cache-header parsing code. Nomnom had a feast.
gist.summary: Consolidated not-billed and content-type guards in the generation tracking middleware and removed unused semantic-cache parsing logic.
gist.impact: Improves internal code maintainability and adds regression tests for non-billed edge cases without affecting the public API.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee organizing files or sweeping up a server room. Nomnom creature eating old code/dead cache headers.
